title: Design Invariant Verification — Code Review

findings.invariant: exact match only, no wildcard logic
findings.evidence: Function implements Candidate F: extracts account ID and role name from assumed-role ARN, returns '<account>#<role-name>'. Fail-closed on non-BedrockUser prefix, BedrockUser-Shared, non-assumed-role ARNs, and empty input. No wildcard, prefix, suffix, contains, regex, or glob matching logic. 11 unit tests pass. Live-verified: derived_principal_id = 107650139384#BedrockUser-cgjang.
findings.code_hash_note: Candidate F implementation — live-verified (single-user, cgjang FSx, session botocore-session-1773807868).

findings.invariant: DynamoDB GetItem with exact key — no scan, no query, no filter
findings.evidence: Uses dynamodb.Table.get_item(Key={'principal_id': principal_id}). DynamoDB GetItem is inherently exact-match on the partition key. No Query, Scan, FilterExpression, or begins_with/contains conditions. A principal_id that does not exactly match a PrincipalPolicy record returns None → deny.

findings.invariant: derived username is NOT used in any enforcement path
findings.evidence: Searched entire handler.py for any username extraction from role name (e.g., stripping 'BedrockUser-' prefix). No such logic exists. The raw principal_id (currently userArn) flows through policy lookup, quota check, daily usage update, idempotency, ledger, session metadata, and approval flow without any derived-username transformation. Derived username is a future display/reporting concern only.

findings.invariant: RequestLedger is immutable — UpdateItem/DeleteItem explicitly denied
findings.evidence: iam.tf contains three DynamoDB policy statements: (1) DynamoDBReadWriteNonLedger — allows GetItem/PutItem/UpdateItem/DeleteItem/Query on all tables EXCEPT RequestLedger. (2) RequestLedgerPutOnly — allows only PutItem on RequestLedger. (3) RequestLedgerDenyMutation — explicit Deny on UpdateItem and DeleteItem for RequestLedger. The explicit Deny overrides any Allow, making the ledger append-only at the IAM level.

findings.invariant: PrincipalPolicy PK is principal_id (String) — exact-match by design
findings.evidence: dynamodb.tf defines principal_policy table with hash_key = 'principal_id', type = 'S'. No range key, no GSI. All lookups are GetItem on the partition key, which is inherently exact-match. No begins_with, contains, or wildcard-capable access pattern is possible via GetItem.

findings.invariant: no wildcard, prefix, suffix, or contains matching anywhere
findings.evidence: Searched handler.py for: startswith, endswith, 'in ', regex, re.match, re.search, fnmatch, glob, begins_with, contains, BETWEEN, FilterExpression. None found in any enforcement path. All DynamoDB operations use exact-key GetItem or exact-key Query (DailyUsage uses principal_id_date composite key, TemporaryQuotaBoost uses principal_id partition key — both exact match).

limitations: Code review only — no live execution for Phases A-D. Real AWS captures require human operator.
normalize_principal_id() Candidate F implemented and live-verified (single-user). Cross-user (C2/C3/C5) deferred.
Cross-environment, cross-role, and fail-closed live validation not yet performed.
